title: Fold-safe target encodings for categorical strategy priors
summary: Add smoothed out-of-fold target encodings for Race, Driver, Compound, Race_Year, and selected interactions to capture stable pit-strategy priors.
rationale: Frequency encodings and group medians for Driver, Race, Compound, and Race_Year can capture useful category priors, but they are weaker than properly fold-safe target encodings. Because target-derived features are leakage-prone, they require explicit out-of-fold construction rather than a generic preprocess(df) implementation.
implementation_hint: Implement fold-safe smoothed target encoding outside preprocess. Generate OOF encodings for Race_Year, Compound, Race_Year x Compound, Driver, Race, and Driver x Compound using fold-training labels only. Use global-prior smoothing and minimum-count thresholds. For test rows, fit encoders on all training labels. First minimal experiment: add only Race_Year x Compound encoding to the step-65 pipeline.
expected_effect: Medium potential improvement in ROC AUC and worst-fold AUC if categorical strategy priors are stable. Confidence is medium-low because implementation risk is high.
risk: Very high leakage risk if implemented naively. Encodings must never use validation labels. Random CV may overstate the effect, so grouped CV is required. The approach may not fit cleanly into the current simple preprocess(df) pattern used by existing AIDE nodes.
